name: Montana Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: 301 S. Park Ave., P.O. Box 200513, Helena MT 59620-0513

requiredDocuments.name: NPDB Self-Query Report
requiredDocuments.description: Required for all applicants. Conducted via the National Practitioner Data Bank.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Out-of-State Licenses
requiredDocuments.description: If licensed in other jurisdictions, you must arrange for official verifications to be sent to Montana.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Montana dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial cost is a $75 application fee.

mainEntity.name: When does the Montana license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Montana dietitian and nutritionist licenses expire biennially. The renewal fee is $100.

mainEntity.name: Why is it through the Board of Medical Examiners?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Montana, the regulation of dietitians and nutritionists is structured under the Board of Medical Examiners, which oversees various allied health professions to ensure public safety in medical nutrition therapy.

mainEntity.name: Does Montana have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Montana. To work in Montana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Montana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
